feed.title: They Said So — Quote of the Day
feed.subtitle: Daily quotes across categories (inspire, life, love, art, management, sports, funny, nature) from theysaidso.com.

entry.title: No student knows his subject: the most he knows is where and how to find out the things he does not know - Woodrow T. Wilson
entry.content: No student knows his subject: the most he knows is where and how to find out the things he does not know - Woodrow T. Wilson

entry.title: The main thing is to be moved, to love, to hope, to tremble, to live. - Auguste Rodin
entry.content: The main thing is to be moved, to love, to hope, to tremble, to live. - Auguste Rodin

entry.title: I came from a real tough neighborhood. Once a guy pulled a knife on me. I knew he wasn't a professional, the knife had butter on it. - Rodney Dangerfield
entry.content: I came from a real tough neighborhood. Once a guy pulled a knife on me. I knew he wasn't a professional, the knife had butter on it. - Rodney Dangerfield

entry.title: Serious sport has nothing to do with fair play. It is bound up with hatred, jealousy, boastfulness, disregard of all rules and sadistic pleasure in witnessing violence. In other words, it is war minus the shooting. - George Orwell
entry.content: Serious sport has nothing to do with fair play. It is bound up with hatred, jealousy, boastfulness, disregard of all rules and sadistic pleasure in witnessing violence. In other words, it is war minus the shooting. - George Orwell

entry.title: No man will make a great leader who wants to do it all himself, or to get all the credit for doing it - Andrew Carnegie
entry.content: No man will make a great leader who wants to do it all himself, or to get all the credit for doing it - Andrew Carnegie

entry.title: Love seems the swiftest, but it is the slowest of growths. No man or woman really knows what perfect love is until they have been married a quarter of a century. - Mark Twain
entry.content: Love seems the swiftest, but it is the slowest of growths. No man or woman really knows what perfect love is until they have been married a quarter of a century. - Mark Twain

entry.title: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing. - Marcus Aurelius
entry.content: The art of living is more like wrestling than dancing. - Marcus Aurelius

entry.title: There's such a thin line between winning and losing. - John R. Tunis
entry.content: There's such a thin line between winning and losing. - John R. Tunis

entry.title: A student never forgets an encouraging private word, when it is given with sincere respect and admiration. - William Lyon Phelps
entry.content: A student never forgets an encouraging private word, when it is given with sincere respect and admiration. - William Lyon Phelps

entry.title: You don't make art out of good intentions. - Gustave Flaubert
entry.content: You don't make art out of good intentions. - Gustave Flaubert

entry.title: I once asked a policeman how far it was to the subway. He said, I don't know, no one has ever made it. - Rodney Dangerfield
entry.content: I once asked a policeman how far it was to the subway. He said, I don't know, no one has ever made it. - Rodney Dangerfield

entry.title: The triple is the most exciting play in baseball. Home runs win a lot of games, but I never understood why fans are so obsessed with them. - Hank Aaron
entry.content: The triple is the most exciting play in baseball. Home runs win a lot of games, but I never understood why fans are so obsessed with them. - Hank Aaron

entry.title: Leadership is a word and a concept that has been more argued than almost any other I know. I am not one of the desk-pounding types that likes to stick out his jaw and look like he is bossing the show. I would far rather get behind and, recognizing the frailties and the requirements of human nature,
entry.content: Leadership is a word and a concept that has been more argued than almost any other I know. I am not one of the desk-pounding types that likes to stick out his jaw and look like he is bossing the show. I would far rather get behind and, recognizing the frailties and the requirements of human nature, would rather try to persuade a man to go along, because once I have persuaded him, he will stick. If I scare him, he will stay just as long as he is scared, and then he is gone. - Dwight David Eisenhower

entry.title: Love many things, for therein lies the true strength, and whosoever loves much performs much, and can accomplish much, and what is done in love is done well. - Vincent Van Gogh
entry.content: Love many things, for therein lies the true strength, and whosoever loves much performs much, and can accomplish much, and what is done in love is done well. - Vincent Van Gogh

entry.title: Life has meaning only if one barters it day by day for something other than itself. - Antoine de SaintExupery
entry.content: Life has meaning only if one barters it day by day for something other than itself. - Antoine de SaintExupery

entry.title: When you win, say nothing. When you lose, say less. - Paul Brown
entry.content: When you win, say nothing. When you lose, say less. - Paul Brown
